hook.text: The Burj Khalifa stands 828 meters tall — nearly twice the Empire State Building.

buildup.text: 12,000 workers placed concrete at 4 AM to avoid the desert heat that could crack it.

discovery.text: The Y-shaped floor plan was designed to reduce wind forces — engineering inspired by a desert flower.

twist.text: Dubai's financial crisis hit during construction. Abu Dhabi bailed out the project at the last moment.

climax.text: The Burj Khalifa opened in 2010 — the tallest structure humans have ever built.

punchline.text: Named after the man who saved it, not the man who built it.

quiz.question: Why was the Burj Khalifa named after Sheikh Khalifa?
options: Abu Dhabi's bailout saved the project during a financial crisis
He was the tallest person in the UAE
